title: Arrays & Array Methods
summary: An array is an ordered list of values.
Each item has a position called an index, starting at zero.
Array methods let you add, remove, and transform items.
Arrays are everywhere in real code.

sections.text: When you need to hold many values together, like a list of names or scores, you use an array. It keeps them in order and lets you work with the whole group at once.

sections.title: What an Array Is
sections.text: An array is an ordered list of values inside square brackets. Each item has a position called an index, and counting starts at zero, so the first item is at index zero. You can read any item by its index.

sections.title: Array Methods
sections.text: Arrays come with built in helpers called methods. Some add or remove items. Others go further, like map to make a new array by transforming each item, or filter to keep only the items that pass a test. These methods are a huge part of daily JavaScript.

media.alt: An array transformed by map into a new array

sections.title: A Numbered Train
sections.text: Think of an array as a train with numbered cars, starting at zero. Each car holds one passenger, the value. You can find a passenger by the car number, add a new car at the end, or walk through every car in order.

media.alt: A numbered train where each car holds one value

sections.title: Working with an Array
items: Create the array with values in square brackets.
Read an item using its index, starting at zero.
Add or remove items with the right method.
Use map to transform every item into a new array.
Use filter to keep only the items you want.

sections.text: Because counting starts at zero, the last item is always at the position one less than the array's length. This trips up nearly every beginner at least once.

terms.definition: The position of an item, starting at zero.
A built in helper that works on the array.
Makes a new array by transforming each item.
Keeps only the items that pass a test.

questions.explanation: An array is an ordered list of values.

questions.text: What index is the first item at?
questions.explanation: Array counting starts at zero, so the first item is at index zero.

options: Keeps only the items that pass a test
questions.explanation: filter keeps only the items that pass the test you give it.
